title: Héctor Tajahuerce Alcalá
text:
Currículum Vitae

Education
Double Degree: Sociology & Political Science / Public Administration | 2023 – 2028 (expected)
Universitat de València | Valencia, Spain
Five-year double degree combining sociological theory and methods with political institutions, public policy, and comparative politics. Coursework encompasses qualitative and quantitative research methods, international relations theory, political analysis, and institutional governance.
Erasmus Exchange Programme | Sep. 2026 – Jun. 2027
Université Libre de Bruxelles (ULB) | Brussels, Belgium
Year-long academic exchange at a leading European research university, with coursework focused on European politics, security studies, and international political affairs.
Baccalauréat – Bilingual French/Spanish Programme | 2009 – 2023
Lycée Français International de Valence | Valencia, Spain
Bilingual secondary education under the French national curriculum. Awarded the French Baccalauréat with honours.


Professional Experience
Short-Term Mobility – Hybrid Warfare & Disinformation | April 2026
FORTHEM Alliance / University of Latvia | Riga, Latvia
Intensive programme on hybrid information warfare, disinformation, cybersecurity, and strategic communication. Included institutional study visits to the Latvian Ministry of Defence and the Cabinet of Ministers, with exposure to Baltic security perspectives and NATO’s eastern flank threat assessments.
Outreach Representative | Summer 2024
UNHCR – United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees | Valencia, Spain
Public representation of UNHCR’s humanitarian mandate through direct outreach campaigns. Developed communication skills in multicultural environments while raising awareness of the global refugee crisis and international protection frameworks.
Internship Observer – University Management | 2024
Universitat Jaume I | Castellón, Spain
Observed and analysed the organisational structures and administrative processes of a Spanish public university, with a focus on institutional governance and faculty management.


Skills & Languages

Languages: Spanish (native), French (native), English (Professional Working Proficiency), Valencian (basic), Portuguese (learning)
Research Methods: Qualitative analysis (Atlas.ti), quantitative analysis (SPSS), policy research and drafting
Technical: Excel (advanced), SPSS (advanced), Python (basic), AI & LLMs
Other: Political analysis, briefing preparation, international affairs monitoring
